hypothesis: In CR2032 Li-metal half-cells with 1 M LiPF6 in EC:DEC (1:1), Si–X alloy anodes exhibiting a Li-ion migration channel radius slope |dRt/dSOC| ≤ 0.45 Å per 100% state-of-charge will undergo <8% electrode thickness change and retain ≥85% capacity after 500 C/2 cycles, whereas alloys with |dRt/dSOC| > 0.45 Å per 100% will fail to meet at least one of these metrics.
